title: Marketing Consulting Services

role_judgments.rationale: Research synthesis, competitive analysis, first-draft positioning and deck production are substantially automatable; client persuasion, workshop facilitation and accountability remain human work.
Internal reporting, coordination and planning automate, while selling, client retention and owner judgment remain concentrated in the human principal.
Research synthesis, segmentation drafts, reporting and routine campaign analysis are highly exposed, while research design and interpretation remain partly human.
Proposal, prospecting and pitch-material preparation automate; relationship selling and referral trust do not transfer cleanly to AI.
Scheduling, status reporting and deliverable tracking automate, while client expectation management and escalation judgment persist.
Routine client queries, scheduling and information retrieval are automatable; sensitive service recovery remains human-led.
Generic analytical, documentation and operational-specialist tasks are exposed, but this catch-all SOC contains work whose task mix is not identifiable here.
AI coding assistance reduces routine development effort, while systems integration, quality assurance and martech implementation remain partly human.
Bookkeeping, reconciliation, invoicing support and routine financial reporting are among the more automatable back-office activities; review and accountability remain.
Screening, documentation and routine communications automate; employee relations and sensitive people decisions remain human.

The unlisted wage share is expected to include content, creative and digital-specialist work, whose production tasks are exposed to generative AI; the composition is not observed and this is deliberately uncertain.

ai_replaceable_share.source_fact: No fetched source provides task-level automatable fractions for the supplied 4-digit 5416 occupation mix or for NAICS 541613.
ai_replaceable_share.derivation: Judgmental task mapping only: each listed SOC is assessed against routine research, drafting, analysis, coordination and back-office tasks versus relationship, accountability and implementation work. No source establishes the selected fractions.

pi_dist.rationale: Marketing consulting commonly sells recurring retainers and projects, so buyers can benchmark the hours and outputs made cheaper by AI at renewal; some value-based and performance-linked work can retain part of the benefit.
pi_dist.source_fact: No fetched source measures the share of AI delivery-cost savings retained by NAICS 541613 firms versus passed through to clients.
pi_dist.derivation: Judgmental estimate. The selected 0.72 gives substantial, but not complete, pass-through because retainers and projects can be repriced while measurable outcomes can preserve some supplier capture. No source establishes this value.

pi_moat.rationale: Outside perspective, vertical familiarity and client trust provide some protection, but there is no license or physical constraint and much strategy, research and creative output is portable or reproducible with client-side AI tools.
pi_moat.source_fact: No fetched source measures a durable AI-era moat for marketing consulting services in NAICS 541613.
pi_moat.derivation: Judgmental estimate balancing relationship and accountability value against substitutable advisory and content outputs. No source establishes the selected value.

t50_years.source_fact: Gartner reported that 27% of CMOs said their marketing organization had "limited or no GenAI adoption in marketing campaigns"; its survey covered 418 marketing leaders from July through September 2024.
t50_years.derivation: For current_adoption_pct, the 73% complement of limited-or-no adoption is haircut by 25% for the marketing-organization-to-marketing-consulting-firm proxy and rounded to 0.55. Because the selected current adoption is at least 0.50, the frozen v3.1 rule requires t50_years=0. The cited survey does not itself measure NAICS 541613 firms or a forecast date.
t50_years.plausible_range: 0 years under the v3.1 boundary if current adoption is at least 50%; otherwise 1-4 years

citations.title: Gartner Survey Reveals Over a Quarter of Marketing Organizations Have Limited or No Adoption of GenAI for Marketing Campaigns
citations.figure_quoted: Twenty-seven percent of CMOs report that their marketing organization has limited or no GenAI adoption in marketing campaigns.
citations.scope: 418 marketing leaders surveyed July-September 2024; marketing organizations, not U.S. NAICS 541613 consulting firms; campaign adoption, not a direct firm-count adoption measure.

current_adoption_pct.source_fact: Gartner reported that 27% of CMOs said their marketing organization had "limited or no GenAI adoption in marketing campaigns"; among organizations that had adopted GenAI, 77% used it for creative development and 48% for strategy development.
current_adoption_pct.derivation: Starting proxy: 1 - 0.27 = 0.73 of surveyed marketing organizations were not in the limited-or-no group. Apply a 0.75 scope haircut for treating client-side marketing organizations as an imperfect proxy for U.S. marketing-consulting firms and for requiring material use in automatable functions: 0.73 × 0.75 = 0.5475, rounded to 0.55. This is not a direct NAICS 541613 firm-adoption observation.

citations.title: Gartner Survey Reveals Over a Quarter of Marketing Organizations Have Limited or No Adoption of GenAI for Marketing Campaigns
citations.figure_quoted: Twenty-seven percent of CMOs report that their marketing organization has limited or no GenAI adoption in marketing campaigns.
citations.scope: 418 marketing leaders surveyed July-September 2024; marketing organizations, not U.S. NAICS 541613 consulting firms; campaign adoption, not a direct firm-count adoption measure.

citations.title: Gartner Survey Reveals Over a Quarter of Marketing Organizations Have Limited or No Adoption of GenAI for Marketing Campaigns
citations.figure_quoted: Of organizations that have adopted GenAI, 77% have adopted it for creative development tasks... 48% of organizations that have adopted GenAI have adopted it for strategy development.
citations.scope: Gartner survey of marketing organizations adopting GenAI; task-use shares among adopters, not a NAICS 541613 firm-adoption measure.

historical_analogs.value: The Spring 2025 CMO Survey reported generative-AI use in marketing activities rose from 7.0% in Spring 2024 to 15.1% in 2025, a stated 116% increase; it is a client-side marketing-activity trend rather than an NAICS 541613 firm outcome.
historical_analogs.source_fact: The report states: "Use of generative AI in marketing increased by 116% over the last year" and shows 7.0% for Spring 2024 and 15.1% for 2025.
historical_analogs.derivation: No numerical transformation; the value reports the fetched survey observation and explicitly retains its population mismatch.
historical_analogs.plausible_range: The observed survey values are 7.0% and 15.1%; applicability to marketing-consulting firms is uncertain.

citations.title: The CMO Survey Highlights and Insights Report, Spring 2025
citations.figure_quoted: Use of generative AI in marketing increased by 116% over the last year ... Spring 2024 7.0% ... 2025 15.1%.
citations.scope: Spring 2025 CMO Survey, company marketing activities; self-reported activity share, not U.S. NAICS 541613 consulting-firm adoption or revenue.

owners_60plus_pct.source_fact: No fetched source establishes the share of NAICS 541613 owners aged 60+.
owners_60plus_pct.derivation: Judgmental estimate for a founder-led professional-service niche. National owner-readiness material was inspected but is not used as a numerical age proxy because it does not report a 541613 60+ share. No source establishes 0.27.

succession_shortage_documented.evidence: No NAICS-541613-specific fetched evidence establishes a shortage of successors. National owner-readiness material documents broad transition pressure but is not treated as a code-specific succession shortage.

active_consolidators.source_fact: Marketbridge announced its acquisition of April Six, a specialist agency, on January 2, 2025. Primer announced it had been acquired by Klimt& Inc on September 4, 2025.
active_consolidators.derivation: Count the two distinct named acquirers with fetched 2025 announcements: Marketbridge and Klimt& Inc. This is a disclosed lower-bound activity count, not a census of all NAICS 541613 consolidators; both targets include agency work outside the code boundary.
active_consolidators.plausible_range: 2-12 materially relevant active acquirers, depending on whether adjacent agencies, global acquirers and unannounced deals are included.

citations.title: Marketbridge acquires April Six to scale growth consulting and marketing services globally
citations.figure_quoted: Marketbridge, a leading integrated growth consulting and marketing firm, today announced the acquisition of April Six.
citations.scope: Announcement dated January 2, 2025 for a growth-consulting/marketing acquirer and a specialist agency with UK and U.S. operations; not a NAICS 541613 market-wide count.

citations.title: Primer Joins Klimt& Inc.
citations.figure_quoted: Sept. 04, 2025 – Primer ... has been acquired by Klimt& Inc.
citations.scope: 2025 announcement concerning a performance-creative and paid-media agency; adjacent to, not a direct census of, U.S. NAICS 541613 marketing consulting.

buy_mult.source_fact: No fetched source establishes an EV/EBITDA purchase multiple after market-rate owner compensation for the specified $1-10M EBITDA band in NAICS 541613.
buy_mult.derivation: Judgmental small-target entry multiple. Adjacent marketing-and-advertising listing data were inspected but report asking-price multiples of seller's discretionary earnings, including owner salary and benefits, so they cannot be normalized to EBITDA without an observed owner-compensation bridge. No source establishes 2.4x EBITDA.

exit_mult.source_fact: No fetched source establishes an EV/EBITDA exit multiple after market-rate owner compensation for a rolled-up NAICS 541613 platform in the specified band.
exit_mult.derivation: Judgmental exit multiple modestly above the entry estimate for a diversified, recurring-revenue platform. It does not reuse seller's-discretionary-earnings listing multiples as EBITDA evidence. No source establishes 3.0x EBITDA.

pricing_structure.value: Adjacent marketing and advertising businesses use a client-services model with recurring retainers, project-based fees, and performance-based arrangements; that mix creates a direct channel for AI-driven delivery savings to be renegotiated, but is not a 541613-only billing-share estimate.
pricing_structure.source_fact: BizBuySell states that these businesses generate revenue "often through recurring retainers, project-based fees, or performance-based arrangements".
pricing_structure.derivation: No numerical transformation. The source description is reported as an adjacent-category pricing-model fact; it does not establish the mix or fee-retention rate for NAICS 541613.
pricing_structure.plausible_range: Retainer, project and performance structures all appear in the adjacent category; their 541613 shares are not established.

citations.title: Marketing and Advertising Businesses For Sale - BizBuySell
citations.figure_quoted: These businesses typically operate on a client-services model ... often through recurring retainers, project-based fees, or performance-based arrangements.
citations.scope: BizBuySell's broad marketing-and-advertising businesses-for-sale category, including agencies and niche B2B marketing consultancies; it is not a NAICS 541613-only billing survey.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: The role-level assessment expects meaningful delivery-task automation, while the adoption proxy reaches the v3.1 50% boundary; Python alone supplies the resulting uplift_pp and scores. The key uncertainty is that adoption comes from client-side marketing organizations, not a firm-count survey of 541613 consultancies.

terminal_value.justification: AI can lower the consultant's research, drafting, reporting and creative-production costs, but client-side marketing teams can also use those same functions. The fetched marketing surveys establish adoption and activity growth, not a direct decline in third-party consulting engagement volume; accountability, specialist context and outside perspective leave residual paid demand, while substitutable deck and content work remains exposed.

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: V uses only role-task judgments and A uses only the marketing-adoption proxy plus the frozen t50 boundary. The two publicly announced acquisitions inform active_consolidators only; they do not enter V or A.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: Adjacent-category retainers, projects and performance arrangements make delivery-time savings observable at renewals or bids. That deflation risk is represented in pi_dist=0.72; pi_moat=0.38 reflects limited protection from relationships, context and accountability rather than a pricing exemption.

top_uncertain_inputs.plausible_range: 0 years if the selected current-adoption proxy is at least 50%; otherwise 1-4 years
top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: The frozen rule makes A=10 at zero, versus A=10/t50 for a positive value; the client-side proxy crossing the 50% boundary is therefore material.
Its position relative to 50% controls the t50 boundary and A.

top_uncertain_inputs.plausible_range: Residual creative/content mix 0.40-0.80 within-role automation; supplied 4-digit mix is not code-specific
top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: The residual is a large wage-share remainder, so its task mix materially changes V_raw and V.
The transaction data available were SDE asking multiples rather than normalized EBITDA, so M is highly sensitive to the unsupported normalization.

reviewer_note: DATASET_MISMATCH: the injected occupation mix is 4-digit NAICS 5416 management consulting, whereas 541613 marketing consulting likely has more marketing, content, creative and digital-specialist labor; the mandatory supplied shares were left unchanged and a RESIDUAL judgment was added for the 47.27% remainder. Heterogeneous boundary: the three operating subsegments considered are (1) marketing strategy/brand positioning and growth planning, scored as the dominant advisory subsegment; (2) research, analytics and campaign-performance consulting; and (3) execution-adjacent creative/content and martech support. No fetched revenue table ranks these three within 541613, so the dominance choice is an analytical boundary rather than a claimed market-share fact. Agency, media-buying, advertising and custom-programming businesses appearing in adjacent sources are not treated as NAICS-541613-only evidence.
